question: Is the final selected answer Option A?
Does the model explicitly identify Process P1 as the one that will finish last?
Does the model correctly identify the presence of three processes (P1, P2, P3) and four resources (R1, R2, R3, R4)?
Does the model correctly describe Resource R4 as having 3 instances (dots) and being isolated (no connected edges)?
Does the model correctly identify that Resource R3 has 1 instance and is allocated to Process P3 (arrow from R3 to P3)?
Does the model correctly identify that Process P2 is requesting Resource R3 (arrow from P2 to R3)?
Does the model correctly identify that Resource R1 has 1 instance and is allocated to Process P2 (arrow from R1 to P2)?
Does the model correctly identify that Process P1 is requesting Resource R1 (arrow from P1 to R1)?
Does the model correctly identify that Process P3 is requesting Resource R2 (arrow from P3 to R2)?
Does the model avoid fabricating non-existent edges, such as connecting Resource R4 to any process?
Does the model correctly distinguish between allocation edges (Resource to Process) and request edges (Process to Resource) in its analysis?
